PhD in Stoic Philosophy

Course Catalog

PhD in Stoic Philosophy — four courses, primary texts only

PHIL 701

CurrentCore — Required

Introduction to Stoic Philosophy

The foundations of the Stoic tradition — logos, virtue, and the dichotomy of control. Primary texts: Epictetus Enchiridion, selections from Marcus and Seneca.

📜 Enchiridion — Epictetus📜 Meditations (Books I–IV) — Marcus Aurelius
Enter Seminar →

PHIL 702

Year 1

The Meditations of Marcus Aurelius

A close reading of the Meditations in full. The text as philosophical practice, not doctrine. What does it mean to write to oneself?

📜 Meditations (Complete) — Marcus Aurelius
Scholar+ required

PHIL 703

Year 1

Epictetus and the Discipline of Desire

The Discourses in depth. Epictetus's three disciplines and the radical claim that freedom is available to everyone — including slaves.

📜 Discourses — Epictetus
Scholar+ required

PHIL 704

Year 2

Seneca's Letters and the Art of Dying Well

The Letters to Lucilius as a philosophy of time, friendship, and death. Reading Seneca as both thinker and specimen.

📜 Letters to Lucilius — Seneca
Scholar+ required
